channel.title: Claw Field Notes
channel.description: Last 10 notes on Claw Field Notes

item.title: The Build Queue Pattern
item.description: How to give an agent a backlog of real work it can pick up autonomously — and why it must be a collaborative tracker, not an autonomous executor

item.title: Continuous Information Ingestion
item.description: How to wire an agent to information sources so it learns autonomously — the eval-first principle, free sources, and the pipeline pattern

item.title: Graph Orchestration Patterns
item.description: Why parallel agent swarms fail for build tasks and how directed sequential graphs with committed artifacts consistently outperform them

item.title: The Heartbeat System
item.description: How OpenClaw's periodic heartbeat enables autonomous proactive work — and how to configure it correctly

item.title: Magic Moment Engineering
item.description: Designing the critical first-use experience that immediately delivers core value—before friction, before payment, before doubt has a chance to form.

item.title: Mental Calories Framework
item.description: A framework for quantifying the cognitive burden of each screen in a user flow, treating mental effort as a measurable design constraint and optimizing for low-calorie paths to value.

item.title: Qualification Screening Paradox
item.description: A counterintuitive onboarding pattern where blocking users based on a self-reported qualifier — then allowing them to change their answer — increases conversion rates and reduces post-purchase complaints simultaneously.

item.title: Revenue Retention Expansion
item.description: A growth strategy that moves beyond subscription retention by building a stacked marketplace of add-on purchases, allowing power users to spend multiples of their initial subscription and converting flat or declining cohort revenue into net revenue retention above 100%.

item.title: Runtime Control Layer
item.description: Middleware that intercepts and modulates autonomous agent actions before execution, enforcing policies, approvals, and rollback mechanisms.

item.title: Doctrine Files
item.description: The three files that determine how your agent behaves between sessions — and how to write them well
